definitions.epsilon_s: low-frequency relative permittivity including lattice polarization; intended for Poisson and electrostatic screening
definitions.epsilon_infinity: high-frequency relative permittivity above optical-phonon response and below electronic absorption; not interchangeable with epsilon_s
definitions.oxide_permittivity: permittivity of a passivation or native oxide; excluded from the bulk HgCdTe electrostatic parameter

source_lineage.title: Reststrahlen spectra of HgTe and CdxHg1-xTe
source_lineage.source_role: primary 77 K and 300 K reststrahlen spectra and phonon-mode evidence
source_lineage.accepted_use: primary endpoint and alloy phonon evidence; Lyddane-Sachs-Teller consistency checks
source_lineage.prohibited_use: claiming that the later composition polynomial was printed by this paper

source_lineage.title: Properties of Mercury Cadmium Telluride
source_lineage.venue: EMIS Datareviews Series No. 3, INSPEC, London
source_lineage.source_role: authoritative compiled-data lineage cited by Tong and Ravindra for composition-dependent low- and high-frequency dielectric data
source_lineage.accepted_use: lineage source for the common benchmark polynomial family
source_lineage.prohibited_use: source-exact coefficient claim until the relevant data-review page is inspected

source_lineage.title: Optical properties of Hg1-xCdxTe
source_lineage.source_role: secondary review connecting Fig. 2 dielectric composition data to Brice-Capper and checking HgTe/CdTe LST ratios against Baars-Sorger
source_lineage.reported_temperature_treatment: composition curves described as average values for 77 K and 300 K
source_lineage.accepted_use: traceability and separation of epsilon_s from epsilon_infinity
source_lineage.prohibited_use: treating review curves as independent primary measurements

static_candidate_family.coefficient_difference_interpretation: transcription-and-lineage bracket only; not a physical uncertainty estimate

benchmark_domain.domain_reason: limited to the primary Baars-Sorger alloy and temperature coverage

high_frequency_lineage_warning.decision: do not use the 5.6 coefficient; retain epsilon_infinity outside the electrostatic closure until its source page is inspected

temperature_decision.reason: later sources state that temperature dependence was not resolved, but no primary uncertainty bound or equation-quality source has been recovered
temperature_decision.benchmark_treatment: one bracket may be used at 77 K and 300 K only, with no extrapolated temperature law

required_before_material_closure: inspect the relevant Brice-Capper 1987 data-review pages and references
recover primary static-permittivity or reststrahlen-derived values with composition and temperature metadata
resolve the 15.5 versus 15.6 static coefficient at the source page
establish physical uncertainty beyond the transcription bracket
verify temperature dependence or justify a bounded temperature-independent approximation
connect the accepted epsilon_s uncertainty to screening and Poisson sensitivity
